summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn preached from the story of Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego in Daniel 3, exploring the tension between cultural pressure and faithful obedience. He framed the king's decree to 'bow down or be burned' as a modern challenge for believers, asking whether we would comply with worldly standards or stand firm in God's truth. The central message was that the 'fires' of life—trials, suffering, and opposition—are not against us but for us, serving God's purpose. Pastor Travis outlined four reasons the fire is for the believer: it proves God's presence, demonstrates His power, prepares us for God's purpose, and deepens our prayer and praise. He emphasized that God is in the fire with us, that His power works best in our weakness, and that trials refine our faith like silver in a furnace. The sermon included practical applications on tithing as a test of putting God first and a call to 'ugly praise'—worshiping God even in the midst of hardship. The closing invitation encouraged listeners to seek first God's kingdom and trust Him through every fire.
mainPoints: The fires of life prove God's presence with us, as He is in the fire with us, just as He was with the three Hebrew men.
The fires of life demonstrate God's power, which works best in our weakness and leaves no negative evidence of the trial.
The fires of life prepare us for God's purpose by refining our character and deepening our dependence on Him through prayer and praise.

versesMentioned.text: Woe to those who call evil good and good evil, who put darkness for light and light for darkness, who put bitter for sweet and sweet for bitter.
Do not conform to the pattern of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind. Then you will be able to test and approve what God’s will is—his good, pleasing and perfect will.
But even if he does not, we want you to know, Your Majesty, that we will not serve your gods or worship the image of gold you have set up."
Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid or terrified because of them, for the LORD your God goes with you; he will never leave you nor forsake you."

When you pass through the waters, I will be with you; and when you pass through the rivers, they will not sweep over you. When you walk through the fire, you will not be burned; the flames will not set you ablaze.

Then King Nebuchadnezzar leaped to his feet in amazement and asked his advisers, "Weren’t there three men that we tied up and threw into the fire?" They replied, "Certainly, Your Majesty." He said, "Look! I see four men walking around in the fire, unbound and unharmed, and the fourth looks like a son of the gods."
Even though I walk through the darkest valley, I will fear no evil, for you are with me; your rod and your staff, they comfort me.
When Jesus spoke again to the people, he said, "I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will never walk in darkness, but will have the light of life."

The satraps, prefects, governors and royal advisers crowded around them. They saw that the fire had not harmed their bodies, nor was a hair of their heads singed; their robes were not scorched, and there was no smell of fire on them.
But he said to me, "My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness." Therefore I will boast all the more gladly about my weaknesses, so that Christ’s power may rest on me.
for our "God is a consuming fire."

John answered them all, "I baptize you with water. But one who is more powerful than I will come, the straps of whose sandals I am not worthy to untie. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire."
See, I have refined you, though not as silver; I have tested you in the furnace of suffering.

"Bring the whole tithe into the storehouse, that there may be food in my house. Test me in this," says the LORD Almighty, "and see if I will not throw open the floodgates of heaven and pour out so much blessing that there will not be room enough to store it."
"When my life was ebbing away, I remembered you, LORD, and my prayer rose to you, to your holy temple."

Monday.scripture: Daniel 3:18 – "But even if he does not... we will not serve your gods."
Monday.focus: Standing firm in faith regardless of the outcome.
Monday.action: Identify one area where cultural pressure conflicts with your faith and make a conscious choice to obey God.
Monday.prayer: God, give me the courage of Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego to be faithful even if You don't deliver me the way I hope.

Tuesday.scripture: Isaiah 43:2 – "When you walk through the fire, you will not be burned."
Tuesday.focus: Trusting in God's presence in the midst of trial.
Tuesday.action: Write down a current 'fire' in your life and acknowledge aloud, "God, You are in this with me."
Tuesday.prayer: Lord, thank You for Your promise to be with me. Help me to sense Your presence in my difficulty today.

Wednesday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 12:9 – "My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness."
Wednesday.focus: Leaning on God's strength in our weakness.
Wednesday.action: Instead of hiding a weakness, share it with a trusted believer and ask them to pray for God's strength to be shown in it.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, I boast in my weakness today so that Your power can be displayed. Let Your grace be enough for me.

Thursday.scripture: Isaiah 48:10 – "I have tested you in the furnace of suffering."
Thursday.focus: Understanding the refining purpose of suffering.
Thursday.action: Reflect on a past trial. Ask God to show you one way it made you more like Him or prepared you for His purpose.
Thursday.prayer: Refiner, help me to see my current struggles as part of Your process to purify me and prepare me for Your plans.

Friday.scripture: Malachi 3:10 – "Test me in this... see if I will not throw open the floodgates of heaven."
Friday.focus: Putting God first through faithful giving.
Friday.action: Review your finances. If you do not tithe, take a step of faith by giving a percentage to God's work this week.
Friday.prayer: Father, I choose to put You first with my resources. I trust Your promise to provide and bless as I am faithful.

Saturday.scripture: Psalm 23:4 – "Even though I walk through the darkest valley, I will fear no evil, for you are with me."
Saturday.focus: Offering 'ugly praise' in the midst of hardship.
Saturday.action: Spend time in worship today, praising God specifically for who He is, even if your circumstances are difficult.
Saturday.prayer: God, I praise You not because my life is perfect, but because You are perfect, faithful, and with me. My hope is in You.
